questions: Does the auto-resolved merge of tickets/index.ts preserve both ticket-side and staging-side changes?
Are there any remaining git conflict markers in the CLI source code?
Does the TypeScript build pass after the merge?

answers.answer: Yes. The merged file at src/tickets/index.ts (150 lines) correctly includes all 10 subcommand imports at lines 4-13 and a complete switch statement handling list, latest, get, create, update-description, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, and bundle. resolveTicket is properly used for ref-based commands. Both ticket-side (library commands integration) and staging-side changes are preserved.
evidence: src/tickets/index.ts:43-148 switch statement with all 10 cases

answers.answer: No. A recursive grep for '<<<<<<<' across src/ returned zero matches. No conflict markers remain in any CLI source file.
Yes. Running tsc --noEmit completed with zero errors, confirming all imports resolve and types are correct after the merge.

statement: The helix-cli merge from staging was auto-resolved cleanly. No conflict markers remain, all 10 subcommands are preserved in tickets/index.ts including the ticket-side library commands, the TypeScript build passes, and no code changes were needed from code review.
